{{Quote|What kind of name is Glomkettle, anyway?|Zayne Carrick|<ref name="kotor10" />}}
'''Glomkettle Hierogryph''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Cadomai Prime/Legends|Cadomai Prime]] and the mother of famed [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]] [[Marn Hierogryph]]<ref name="kotorcg" /> and [[Marn Hierogryph's brother|another son]] who went on to become an [[Actor/Legends|actor]].<ref name="kotor47">[[Knights of the Old Republic 47|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 47]]</ref> [[Marriage/Legends|Married]] to [[Garl Hierogryph]],<ref name="handbook">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]''</ref> Glomkettle often had trouble telling her two sons apart.<ref name="laborpains">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20080409/index.html|text="Labor Pains"|int=Labor Pains|newhost=Suvudu}}</ref> After Marn was involved in a number of incidents in his youth—the last straw being the [[racketeering]] conviction of his [[Marn Hierogryph's tutor|tutor]] on evidence planted by Marn—Glomkettle and Garl Hierogryph sent their son away from Cadomai.<ref name="handbook" /> In [[3964 BBY]], when Marn was involved in simulating a fake [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] attack on the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]]-held [[Flashpoint Stellar Research Station]], the con artist called his imagined [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] the ''Glomkettle''.<ref name="kotor10" />

Later, [[Zayne Carrick]] used "Glomkettle" as an alias when he impersonated a Mandalorian commander on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] to rescue a group of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|younglings]] from the leader of the [[Mandalorian Knights]], [[Dorjander Kace]].<ref name="kotorw5">[[Knights of the Old Republic: War 5|''Knights of the Old Republic: War'' 5]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Glomkettle Hierogryph was first mentioned in [[Knights of the Old Republic 10|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 10]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released in [[2006]].<ref name="kotor10" /> More information on Hierogryph was given in Miller's short story ''[[Labor Pains]]'',<ref name="laborpains" /> as well as the reference books ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' and ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''.<ref name="kotorcg" />
